label: Process supervision & step-level reward models

node.label: Process supervision & step-level reward models
node.definition: Process reward models score intermediate reasoning steps or partial trajectories rather than judging only a final answer.
node.why_it_matters: Dense feedback can guide search and learning away from locally plausible but invalid paths.
limitations: Step labels are expensive, verifier errors can be amplified, and locally approved steps can still hide global flaws.

dependencies.rationale: Process supervision & step-level reward models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Reward modeling.
Process supervision & step-level reward models uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Process supervision & step-level reward models uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Process supervision & step-level reward models uses Preference learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
